source_pr.title: test: stabilize test case
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #21912 #17991

Problem Summary:
If the lock is resolved and no lock error reportes, the test may panic like this:
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] PANIC: pessimistic_test.go:657: testPessimisticSuite.TestInnodbLockWaitTimeout
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] 
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] ... Panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference (PC=0x1392B31)
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] 
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] /usr/local/go/src/runtime/panic.go:679
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z]   in gopanic
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] /usr/local/go/src/runtime/panic.go:199
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z]   in panicmem
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] /usr/local/go/src/runtime/signal_unix.go:394
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z]   in sigpanic
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] pessimistic_test.go:739
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z]   in testPessimisticSuite.TestInnodbLockWaitTimeout
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z] /usr/local/go/src/reflect/value.go:321
[2020-12-23T18:27:10.707Z]   in Value.Call


### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
The default set for test 300ms is a bit short, try increasing the `ManagedLockTTL` in the test so that the lock will not be resolved testing with TiKV.

How it Works:

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


Side effects



### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- `No release note`

symptoms: Panic (nil pointer dereference) in TestInnodbLockWaitTimeout when the managed lock is resolved unexpectedly under TiKV.
failure_signature: Panic (nil pointer dereference) in TestInnodbLockWaitTimeout when the managed lock is resolved unexpectedly under TiKV.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing sensitivity and unsafe concurrency in the test code. With TiKV enabled, a short `tikv.ManagedLockTTL` (300ms) could allow the lock to be resolved earlier than expected, leading to a nil-pointer panic in the lock-wait-timeout test. The test also performed assertions inside goroutines, which is not safe for gocheck and can race. This PR stabilizes the test by temporarily increasing `tikv.ManagedLockTTL` during the test (and restoring it), and by collecting goroutine errors via a channel and asserting them in the main goroutine.
fix_pattern: Increase `tikv.ManagedLockTTL` during the lock-wait tests (restore after), and avoid gocheck assertions in goroutines by sending errors to a channel and asserting in the main goroutine.
